user_query:
I need a decision-first team memory pack and weekly review from our last 14 days of work. Please read all of the following files under input/ and use them as the only sources:
- input/git_log.txt (14-day git history for our internal tooling project)
- input/journal.jsonl (journal entries; one JSON object per line with timestamp, tags, and text)
- input/backlog.csv (backlog items with priority, owner, and a "Proposed Decision" column)
- input/tags.yaml (the canonical tag whitelist you must use)
- input/team_profile.json (team name and a recipient DID for sharing)

Deliverables (write all outputs to the paths below using only relative workspace paths):
1) output/plan.md — Before doing anything else, draft a concise plan with: Goal, Constraints, Ordered Steps (with what you will do and which files they touch), Verification Points, and Possible Blockers. Then follow your plan and update it if your approach changes.
2) output/decisions.json — A consolidated decision register as an array of objects. Requirements:
   - Each decision: { id, title, choice, reason, tags:[], model:"A"|"B"|"C", confidence (0..1), sources:[{type:"git"|"journal"|"backlog", ref:string}] }.
   - Extract and deduplicate real decisions from git_log.txt and journal.jsonl (mark these inferred as model "B" if inferred from patterns/commits, model "A" if the decision is explicit in notes/backlog). Add at least two forward-looking predicted decisions (model "C") with confidence values and brief rationale.
   - Minimum totals: at least 6 decisions overall, with at least 3 model "B", at least 1 model "A", and at least 2 model "C".
   - All tags must be drawn from input/tags.yaml only (no new tags). Include at least one source record per decision, citing the file and a brief locator (e.g., commit hash snippet, journal timestamp, or backlog row identifier).
3) output/reflection_weekly.md — A weekly reflection that synthesizes insights from the decisions and notes. Include clearly labeled sections: Highlights, Patterns, Open Questions, Risks, and Next Week Focus. Reference specific decision IDs from output/decisions.json in-line where appropriate.
4) output/graph.json — A small knowledge graph with two arrays: nodes and edges. Nodes should include types "decision", "tag", and "source". Edges should express at least DECISION_HAS_TAG and DECISION_HAS_SOURCE relations. Include every decision from decisions.json and connect them to their tags and sources.
5) output/trade_manifest.json — A shareable manifest to exchange this memory pack with our partner. Use the recipient DID from input/team_profile.json. Include: { recipient_did, ttl_days (3–14), usage_rights (string), offered_items:[{type:"file", path:"output/..."}] }. Offer at least output/decisions.json and output/reflection_weekly.md. Use only relative paths under output/.
6) output/summary.md — An executive summary (plain text is fine) with: total decisions, counts by model (A/B/C), number of tags used, knowledge graph node/edge counts, and 3–5 line rationale for the two most important predicted decisions.

Important rules:
- Use only tags listed in input/tags.yaml.
- Use only data from the provided input files; cite sources for every non-predicted decision.
- Use only relative workspace paths (e.g., input/... and output/...). Do not include any absolute or machine-specific paths.
- Make sure the predicted decisions (model C) are plausible extensions of the patterns you observe in git/journal/backlog, and include confidence values and short rationales.
- Ensure all deliverables are well-structured and consistent with each other (e.g., decision IDs in reflection and graph match decisions.json).
